results.title: They yassified video games
results.content:
### WHAT’S HOT

# They yassified video games

Nvidia just unveiled DLSS 5, and the reaction was not wow, more frames. It was more like why does this game suddenly look like it got an AI skincare routine.

DLSS 5 is Nvidia’s new neural rendering push, arriving this fall, and the pitch is way bigger than upscaling. Instead of just making games run smoother, it adds photoreal lighting and materials in real time, with Nvidia framing it as a step toward Hollywood-level visuals in actual gameplay. Support is planned from publishers including Bethesda, Capcom, Ubisoft, Warner Bros. Games, and more. [...] The backlash showed up immediately because some of the demos made characters look weirdly polished, overcooked, and kind of off. The complaint is not really that it looks fake. It is that it looks like the game’s art direction got run through an aggressive AI beauty filter.

Nvidia says developers will be able to tune the effect with controls for intensity, color grading, and masking, so this is not supposed to be one giant gloss layer slapped on everything. But that has not stopped the early vibe from settling into one very internet-ready argument: Nvidia thinks it built the future of graphics, and a lot of players think it built a slop machine.

#### Reply

We see AI tech as it emerges, and drop it in a newsletter for you.

The Frontier by Product Hunt [...] The Frontier by Product Hunt logo

### Social & Community [...] Key Features:

- Generates hundreds of frames in a single pass

- Works directly for novel view synthesis OR as pseudo-supervision to improve 3D representation

- Can reconstruct from text prompts alone (like its base model)

- Starts from renderings (not pure noise), generating plausible visuals in <4 steps

- Three variants: ArtiFixer (sharper), ArtiFixer3D (more consistent), ArtiFixer3D+ (restores sharpness + consistency)

Benefits:

- Outperforms all baselines by 1–3 dB PSNR on MipNeRF 360, DL3DV, and Nerfbusters

- Generates plausible reconstructions where existing approaches fail completely

- More efficient than costly iterative distillation processes

results.title: D5 Render Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Use color grading LUTs for filmic or stylized tones.

Tune Sharpness slightly (too much looks fake).

If exterior — reduce fog intensity for clarity.

⚙️ 6. Performance Optimization

For smoother real-time preview, lower quality temporarily (e.g., “High”) while adjusting.

Use DLSS / Ray Tracing optimization if your GPU supports it.

#### vs Alternatives

Enscape3D
Lumion
Twinmotion

1. It’s Built for Architects — Not Gamers

Unlike Unreal or Blender’s Cycles, D5 Render is tailored specifically for architectural visualization.

That means:

You don’t waste time tweaking complex lighting setups or shader nodes.

Everything — from daylight, vegetation, materials, to camera — is designed to feel like a real-world scene setup. [...] #### vs Alternatives

Lumion

The quality of the renders provided by D5 is far superior than those provided by Lumion and Twinmotion. Also viewing and working with realtime renders is more helpful and efficient which isnt achieved yet by the other two softwares mentioned

Marix Panaguiton

#### What's great

D5 Render is truly impressive, it’s very user-friendly and easy to navigate. The AI features are a game-changer, making the workflow faster, smoother, and so much more convenient. It really helps bring projects to life with ease!

#### What needs improvement

It would be great to see even more AI-driven tools for material and lighting adjustments, plus continued expansion of the asset library. Slight optimization for heavier scenes would also make the experience even better. [...] #### vs Alternatives

I chose D5 Render because it combines real-time speed with high-quality results. It’s intuitive, efficient, and helps me turn my ideas into stunning visuals in no time.

Kaberia Mwongo

#### What's great

It's great and easy to use. You get great lighting.

Even the free version has a lot of potential, and the output is on par in comparison to other expensive software, which speaks a lot about the company. I highly recommend using it for students or small studios as the asset library is the main reason I love it. The new AI tools allow you to do so much more with so little effort.

#### What needs improvement

#### vs Alternatives

It's great and easy to use. You get great lighting.

results.title: Text your AI from anywhere - Product Hunt
results.content:
🔥 Our Take: Mobile builders have made it much easier to make an app. They have not made it much easier to launch one properly. The bottleneck just moved downstream into store copy, screenshot sets, pricing by market, and all the other stuff that decides whether your app looks legit or half-finished. That is the gap Forvibe is going after.

### Top forum threads [...] ## The email client Product Hunt actually uses

Newsletter Sponsor

Most of us here switched to Superhuman and stopped losing mornings to inbox. AI writes replies, summarizes threads, and triages in seconds. Email and calendar in oneplace, so you're not piecing together context from four different apps.

Fast in a way that actually shows up in your day.

## Stop re-explaining your tools to every new agent

UseAgents is a real-time registry for tools and APIs. Developers define a tool once, and agents can discover and use it without scraping docs, guessing what exists, or relying on stale model knowledge.
